hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' | { read -r f; [[ "$f" == *.kt ]] || exit 0; tail -c1 "$f" | xxd | grep -q '0a' || printf '\n' >> "$f"; wc=$(grep -nE '^import .*\.\*' "$f" | grep -v 'java\.util\.\*' | head -3); fc=$(grep -nE '(TODO|FIXME|STOPSHIP):' "$f" | head -3); ec=$(grep -nE 'catch[[:space:]]*\([^)]+:[[:space:]]*(Exception|RuntimeException|Error|Throwable|NullPointerException|IndexOutOfBoundsException|IllegalMonitorStateException)[[:space:]]*\)|throw[[:space:]]+(Exception|RuntimeException|Error|Throwable)[^A-Za-z0-9_]' "$f" | head -3); [[ -z "$wc$fc$ec" ]] && exit 0; msg="Code quality issues in $f --"; [[ -n "$wc" ]] && msg="$msg WildcardImport: $wc;"; [[ -n "$fc" ]] && msg="$msg ForbiddenComment: $fc;"; [[ -n "$ec" ]] && msg="$msg GenericException: $ec;"; jq -n --arg c "$msg" '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":$c}}'; } 2>/dev/null || true
hooks.statusMessage: Checking .kt file for code quality issues
